back: I want to go home. –고 싶다 : desire
I'm watching a movie. –고 있다 : progressive
I'm going to school tomorrow, too. –(으)ㄹ 거예요 : probable future
Where should we go this weekend? –(으)ㄹ 까요 : wondering/asking question
Will Thomas open the store? –(으)ㄹ 까요 : wondering/asking question
What do you want to drink? -(으)ㄹ 래요 : intention Similar with 거예요 but cant be used to third person
I will study hard. -(으)ㄹ 게요 : willingness Only used by first person (only used by I)
I'll go first. -(으)ㄹ 게요 : willingness Only used by first person (only used by I)
Paul jogs everyday, right? –지요 ● Seeking agreement (rising intonation)
What time is Peter coming back? –지요 Asking a question, believing hearer has the answer (rising intonation)
Where was the key? –지요 Asking a question, believing hearer has the answer (rising intonation)
Yes, I'm leaving tomorrow. (떠나다 : leave) –지요 Assuring information (falling intonation)
Yes, I had an appointment yesterday. –지요 Assuring information (falling intonation)
It's cold, how about closing the window? –지요 Suggesting (falling intonation)
How about practice after lunch(or something). –지요 Suggesting (falling intonation)
Professor, let's go. –지요 Suggesting (falling intonation) + honorific suffix (으)시
Please lend me your book. (빌리다:to lend) –지요 Suggesting (falling intonation) + honorific suffix (으)시
John speaks Korean well. –네요 : speakers unexpected surprise or sudden realization
Thomas was an English teacher. –네요 : speakers unexpected surprise or sudden realization
I guess food may be spicy(맵다). –네요 : speakers unexpected surprise or sudden realization 겠네요 : speaker realization of what will happen (I guess…something)may/will
I guess tomorrow weather may be cold(춥다). –네요 : speakers unexpected surprise or sudden realization 겠네요 : speaker realization of what will happen (I guess…something)may/will
I guess the movie must have been interesting. –네요 : speakers unexpected surprise or sudden realization -었/았겠네요 : I guess…something must have
You must have gone home because it was your sister's birthday. –네요 : speakers unexpected surprise or sudden realization -었/았겠네요 : I guess…something must have
Korean food is delicious. –군요 : immediate realization what speaker just realize -군요 : after adjective or copula stems
So this is John's house. –군요 : immediate realization what speaker just realize -군요 : after adjective or copula stems
(oh I see that) He learns golf everyday. –군요 : immediate realization what speaker just realize -는군요 : after verb stems
(oh I see that) the movie was interesting -었/았군요 : immediate realization what speaker just realize
You must be majoring in Korean literature. (전공 : major) -겠군요 : realization of future event
Try (doing something)
Continue to
Continue to
Do for (someone)
Do all the way
Have finished
Finish up/end up with
Do for later
Be in the state of
Become
End up doing
Action still persists
tried eating kimchi –어/아 보다 : try (doing something)
tried eating Korean food –어/아 보다 : try (doing something)

front: 지금까지 혼자 잘 살 아 왔어요 .
back: Until now, I've lived alone fine –어/아 오다 : continuous action toward the present

front: 저희는 선생님 말씀을 믿 어 왔어요 .
back: We have believed the teachers word. –어/아 오다 : continuous action toward the present
The drama is coming to an end. –어/아 가다 : continuous action goes into future
How's your business going? –어/아 가다 : continuous action goes into future
I read a book in a day. –어/아 내다 : do all the way to the very end
Eventually, he made an electric bulb. –어/아 내다 : do all the way to the very end
Eventually, I found the wallet. –어/아 내다 : do all the way to the very end
Naomi finally went to America. -어/아 버리다 : sense of regret

front: 제 남동생이 벌써 다 먹 어 버렸어요
back: My brother already ate them all. -어/아 버리다 : sense of regret
My sister went back to Korea. –고 말다 : ended up doing

front: 차 안에서 잠이 들 고 말았어요 .
back: I ended up falling asleep in the car. –고 말다 : ended up doing
I ended up going to bed late. –고 말다 : ended up doing
I'll open the door for you. -어/아 주다 : do something as a favor Similar with 어/아 드리다
Laura will buy a bag for me. -어/아 주다 : do something as a favor Similar with 어/아 드리다
I turn on electric lamp in living room (and its still on). (겨다 : turn on (electric lamp)) –어/아 놓다 ( or –어/아 두다 ) : ● continuation of a certain action of state after action
Peter left the door open. –어/아 놓다 ( or –어/아 두다 ) : ● continuation of a certain action of state after action

front: 조깅을 하기 전에 물을 많이 마 셔 놓았어요 .
back: I drank lot of water before jogging for later. –어/아 놓다 ( or –어/아 두다 ) : ● Doing something for later
I've practiced a lot. –어/아 놓다 ( or –어/아 두다 ) : ● Doing something for later
My sister left breakfast for me. (차리다 : prepare (for breakfast)) –어/아 놓다 ( or –어/아 두다 ) : ● Doing something for later

front: 토미가 의자에 앉아요. 토미가 의자에 앉고 있어요. 토미가 의자에 앉 아 있어요 .
back: Tommy sits on chair. Tommy is sitting on the chair. Tommy is seated on the chair. –어/아 있다 : state brought by action on the main verb persists
Still lying down on the bed. –어/아 있다 : state brought by action on the main verb persists
I'm in Rome now. –어/아 있다 : state brought by action on the main verb persists
Dislike –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Like –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
hate –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Fear –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Suffer –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Rejoice –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Grieve –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Envy –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Feel tired –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Hold dear –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Pity –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Want –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Feel cold –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
Feel hot –어/아 하다 : for describing third person feeling
It's getting cold. –어/아지다 : become/begin to be/get to be
The room will become clean. –어/아지다 : become/begin to be/get to be
I go to post office to send mail. (부치다 : send (mail) –(으)러 : the purpose of speaker action/to Usually teamed with 오다 or 가다
I go to the train station to meet my friend. –(으)러 : the purpose of speaker action/to Usually teamed with 오다 or 가다
I went to Starbucks for coffee. –(으)러 : the purpose of speaker action/to Usually teamed with 오다 or 가다

front: 영어 교사로 일하 려고 학위를 땄어요.
back: I got my degree to work as an English teacher. (교사 : instructor; 학위 : degree; 학위 논문 : degree paper) -(으)려고 : speaker intention or plan 따다 3.(경기·내기에서 이겨) win

front: 유럽을 여행하 려고 돈을 모으고 있어요
back: I am saving money to travel in Europe. –(으)려고 : speaker intention or plan

front: 내년에 새 차를 사 려고 해요
back: I intend to buy new car next year. –(으)려고 : speaker intention or plan

front: 좋은 학교에 들어가 려고 열심히 공부했습니다.
back: I studied hard to get into a good school. –(으)려고 : speaker intention or plan

front: 나무가 잘 자라 도록 돌봐 주셨어요.
back: He took care of the tree so that it would grow well. –도록 : so that/ to the point where
We laughed so that we cried. –도록 : so that/ to the point where

front: 우리팀이 게임을 이기 도록 열심히 웅원하고 있습니까?
back: Are you trying hard to win the game? ((이기다 : win) are they cheering so enthusiasticly so that our team may win?) –도록 : so that/ to the point where

front: 너무 기뻐 서 메리한테 전화하고 있어요
back: Because I am so happy, I am calling Mary. –어/아서 : so/because/since

front: 가격이 너무 비싸 서 못 사요.
back: I can't buy it because it's too expensive. –어/아서 : so/because/since

front: 다음 주에 시험이 있어 서 바빠요
back: I'm busy because I have an exam next week. –어/아서 : so/because/since

front: 백화점에 가 서 생일 카드를 살 거예요?
back: Are you going to the department store to buy a birthday card? –어/아서 : action of the first clause occurs before main clause (and – but connecting and)

front: 오늘 피터 집에 가 서 저녁을 먹읍시다.
back: Let's go to Peter's house for dinner today. –어/아서 : action of the first clause occurs before main clause (and – but connecting and)
The coffee is hot, so take your time. -(으)니까 : because, since, so
The coffee is hot, so take your time. -(으)니까 : because, since, so

front: 밤 새 컴퓨터 게임을 하 느라고 학교에 못 갔어요.
back: I couldn't go to school because I was playing computer games all night. –느라고 : because, since, so (but undesirable result)

front: 늦게 일어나 느라고 아침을 못 먹어요.
back: I can't eat breakfast because I wake up late. –느라고 : because, since, so (but undesirable result)

front: 학생이 면 /이라 면 제 방으로 보내세요.
back: If there are students, send them to my room. –(으)면 : if/when

front: 오늘 저녁으로 한국 음식 먹 으면 연락하세요.
back: Call me if you eat Korean food for dinner tonight. –(으)면 : if/when
The movie will be over at 3 o'clock. –(으)면 : if/when

front: 생일 파티를 할 거 면 집에서 하세요.
back: If you're having a birthday party, do it at home. –(으)면 : if/when

front: 토마스가 커피를 시키 면 제가 콜라를 시킬게요.
back: If Thomas orders coffee, I'll order coke. –(으)면 : if/when

front: 이번 크리스마스에 눈이 많이 오 면 해요 .
back: I hope it snows a lot this Christmas. –(으)면 하다/좋겠다 : wish/hope

front: 내년에 새 컴퓨터를 사 면 좋겠어요 .
back: I'd like to buy a new computer next year. –(으)면 하다/좋겠다 : wish/hope

front: 이번 여름에 일본을 여행했 으면 좋겠어요 .
back: I want to travel to Japan this summer. –(으)면 하다/좋겠다 : wish/hope

front: 학교에 다니 려면 먼저 둥록금을 내세요.
back: If you want to go to school, pay the bill first. –(으)려면 : if one intends to do

front: 교수님을 만나 려면 미리 전화해야 돼요.
back: if u intend to meet professor, call in advance –(으)려면 : if one intends to do
The more I eat kimchi the more delicious. –(으)ㄹ수록 : the more,,,the more
The more I sleep, the more tired I get. –(으)ㄹ수록 : the more,,,the more
The more I see the dog, the cuter he is. –(으)ㄹ수록 : the more,,,the more

front: 학교에 가 야 교수님을 만날수 있어요.
back: If you go to school you can meet the professor. –어/아야 : prerequisite or necessary condition of main clause/ only if

front: 열쇠를 찾아 야만 집에 갈 수 있어요.
back: We can only go home if we find the key. –어/아야 : prerequisite or necessary condition of main clause/ only if + 만 (only) for more empathic
If you're not busy, call me. –거든 : ending clause is the condition for the main clause/ if Only for imperative or propositive

front: 시험이 끝나 거든 같이 저녁 먹읍시다
back: Let's have dinner together when the test is over. –거든 : ending clause is the condition for the main clause/ if Only for imperative or propositive

front: 가 : 또 한 잔 시키세요? 나 : 제가 여기 커피를 좋아하 거든요 .
back: Do you want another drink? I like coffee here. –거든 : -거든요 (sentence ending – you know?)

front: 가 : 피곤해 보여요. 나 : 어제 밤 늦게까지 일했 거든요 .
back: You look tired. I worked late last night (you know). –거든 : -거든요 (sentence ending – you know?)
I ate breakfast and then went to post office. –고 : connect two different clauses or “and”
Yesterday weather was cloudy and cold. –고 : connect two different clauses or “and”

front: 필립은 경제학을 전공하 며 찰스는 심리학을 전공합니다.
back: Philip majors in economics and Charles major in psychology. –(으)며 : only non sequential actions. (only in writing)

front: 리사는 재즈를 좋아하 며 제임스는 클래식을 좋아해요.
back: Lisa likes jazz and James likes classical music. –(으)며 : only non sequential actions. (only in writing)

front: 오늘은 바람이 많이 불 며 춥겠습니다.
back: It will be windy and cold today. –(으)며 : only non sequential actions. (only in writing)

front: 교수님의 강의를 들 으며 노트를 적었어요.
back: I took notes while listening to the lecture. –(으)며 : only non sequential actions. (only in writing)

front: 토요일에는 보통 친구들하고 영화를 보 거나 집에서 쉬어요.
back: On Saturdays, I usually watch movies or take a rest at home with my friends. –거나 : used to list two or more action/states /or

front: 아프 거나 피곤할 때 집 생각해요?
back: When you're sick or tired do you think about home? –거나 : used to list two or more action/states /or

front: 커피를 마시 든지 아이스크림을 먹든지 뭔가 시킵시다
back: Let's order coffee or ice cream or something. –든지 : list selection/unenthusiastically about the selection/indifferent

front: 그 옷이 비싸든지 싸 든지 꼭 사 주세요.
back: Please buy the dress whether it's expensive or not –든지 : list selection/unenthusiastically about the selection/indifferent

front: 이 집이 크 면서 학교에서도 가까워요.
back: (While) This house is big, it's close to the school. (what? Just go with it) (으)면서 : while
Are you driving while on the phone? (으)면서 : while
I will go home as soon as finish work. -자마자 : as soon as/ immediately after
I cried as soon as read the letter. -자마자 : as soon as/ immediately after
On my way to school, I stopped by the supermarket. –다가 : express shift in action or state
The taste was watery and then salty. –다가 : express shift in action or state

front: 오전까지 날씨가 좋 다가 지금은 흐립니다
back: The weather was fine until morning, but now it's cloudy. –다가 : express shift in action or state

front: 매일 술을 마시 다가는 건강을 해칠 수 있어요
back: Drinking every day can hurt your health. -다가는 : warn about the action or state of the prior clause.

front: 추운 날씨에 밖에서 오래 운동하 다가는 감기 걸릴 수 있어요
back: If you exercise for a long time in cold weather, you could catch a cold. -다가는 : warn about the action or state of the prior clause.
I was going to go home, but I went to the bookstore. -(으)려다가 : combination of intending to (으려고) : tries to do something but end up doing another

front: 도서관에 가 려다가 길에서 제임스를 만나서 같이 저녁을 먹었어요.
back: I was going to the library and met James on the street and we had dinner together. -(으)려다가 : combination of intending to (으려고) : tries to do something but end up doing another
What were you going to sell but couldn't sell? -(으)려다가 : combination of intending to (으려고) : tries to do something but end up doing another

front: 오늘 밤 날씨가 추 운데 밖에 나가지마세요.
back: Don't go out tonight because it's cold. –는/(은)ㄴ데 : provide background for main clause

front: 이번주에 약속이 있 는데 다음 주가 어때요?
back: I have an appointment this week, so how about next week? –는/(은)ㄴ데 : provide background for main clause
I'm busy today, but let's meet tomorrow. –는/(은)ㄴ데 : provide background for main clause
small happy Adjective verb : (으)ㄴ데

front: 가 : 내일 파티에 같이 갈래요? 나 : 이번 주는 바 쁜데요
back: Would you like to go to the party tomorrow? I'm busy this week... -는/(으)ㄴ 데요 : as a sentence ender (but still background information..for something unspoken?)
The rent is expensive even though it's small. –는/(은)ㄴ데도 : although/despite/even if
It's the weekend, but we don't have many customers. –는/(은)ㄴ데도 : although/despite/even if

front: 눈이 많이 오 지만 안 추워요
back: It snows a lot, but it's not cold. –지만 : but/although

front: 누나는 노래를 잘 하 지만 , 저는 잘 못해요.
back: My sister is good at singing, but I'm not good at it. –지만 : but/although

front: 열심히 공부했 지만 A 를 못 받았어요.
back: I studied hard, but I didn't get an A. –지만 : but/although
It's expensive, but it has many functions. –(으)나 : indicate content of the first clause does not comply with main clause/athough

front: 아침을 먹었 으나 여전히 배가 고팠어요.
back: I had breakfast, but I was still hungry. –(으)나 : indicate content of the first clause does not comply with main clause/athough

front: 매일 연습 해도 실력이 안 늘어요.
back: My skills don't improve even though I practice every day. –어/아도 : but/although
Please come even if you wake up late. –어/아도 : but/although
Even though I'm tired, I'm happy. –어/아도 : but/although
Can I go home now? –어/아도 되다 / 괜찮다/좋다 : (permission) is it alright if I ..
Is it ok even if it's a long trip? –어/아도 되다 / 괜찮다/좋다 : (permission) is it alright if I ..

front: 너무 비싸면 그 책을 안 사 도 돼요 .
back: You don't have to buy the book if it's too expensive. –어/아도 되다 / 괜찮다/좋다 : (permission) is it alright if I ..
You shouldn't lie. –(으)면 안 되다 : you should not/must not

front: 여기에 쓰레기를 버리 면 안 됩니다 .
back: You shouldn't throw garbage away here. –(으)면 안 되다 : you should not/must not
Don't be late for class. –(으)면 안 되다 : you should not/must not
Just study hard. –(으)면 되다 : it would do if/ all you have to do is
As long as the weather is nice. –(으)면 되다 : it would do if/ all you have to do is

front: 아침에 일찍 일어나지 않 으면 안 돼요 .
back: you must get up early in the morning 안 ~(으)면 안 되다 / -지 않으면 안 되다 : obligation or necessity

front: 시험 공부를 하지 않 으면 안 돼요 .
back: I must study for the test. 안 ~(으)면 안 되다 / -지 않으면 안 되다 : obligation or necessity

front: 매일 밤 약을 먹어 야 돼요 .
back: You must take medicine every night. –어/아야 되다 : obligation or necessity/ ( -어/아야 하다 ) more formal
You have to be Korean. –어/아야 되다 : obligation or necessity/ ( -어/아야 하다 ) more formal
The house has to be big. –어/아야 되다 : obligation or necessity/ ( -어/아야 하다 ) more formal

front: 요즘 매일 아침 조깅을 하 는 것 같아요 .
back: It looks like he jogs every morning nowadays. –는 것 같다 : it seems/looks like - present meaning only for verbs

front: 버스가 곧 도착하 는 것 같아요 .
back: It looks like the bus will arrive soon. –는 것 같다 : it seems/looks like - present meaning only for verbs

front: 이 차가 더 좋 은 것 같아요
back: I think this car is better. (으)ㄴ 것 같다 : past meaning or for adjectives, copula ( 이다, 아니다 )

front: 시험 공부를 많이 한 것 같아요
back: It seems like he studied a lot for the test. (으)ㄴ 것 같다 : past meaning or for adjectives, copula ( 이다, 아니다 )
I think I will go to Korea next year. (으)ㄹ 것 같다 : future meaning

front: 친구가 파티에 안 올 것 같아요 .
back: I don't think my friend will come to the party. (으)ㄹ 것 같다 : future meaning
I guess I don't have a key. –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄹ 모양이다 : appears/looks like
it appears that they could not eat breakfast –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄹ 모양이다 : appears/looks like
It looks like he quit smoking. –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄹ 모양이다 : appears/looks like
I seems like summer is here. –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄹ 둣하다 : seems/appears/looks like
He seems like a good man. –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄹ 둣하다 : seems/appears/looks like
I guess she drinks a lot of coffee. –나 보다 : guess/seem  for verbs & past tense
I guess he has a lot of money. –나 보다 : guess/seem  for verbs & past tense

front: 어제 많이 피곤 했 나 봐요 .
back: You seemed very tired yesterday. –나 보다 : guess/seem  for verbs & past tense
The bag seems small. –(으)ㄴ가 보다 : guess/seem  for adjectives
It seems cold outside. –(으)ㄴ가 보다 : guess/seem  for adjectives
The clothes look expensive. –어/아 보이다 : speaker opinion regarding the appearance of something
You looked upset. –어/아 보이다 : speaker opinion regarding the appearance of something

front: 우체국으로 가 는 길에 어머니를 만났어요
back: I met my mother on my way to the post office. –는 길 : on the way, usually with ( 가다 or 오다 )

front: 공항에서 오 는 길에 선물을 살 거예요.
back: I will buy a present on my way from the airport. –는 길 : on the way, usually with ( 가다 or 오다 )
I never learned to play golf. –(으)ㄴ 적/일 : has an/no experience to do something ( 있아 / 없다 )
I haven't met the teacher. –(으)ㄴ 적/일 : has an/no experience to do something ( 있아 / 없다 ) Usually + -어/아 보다
Have you ever worked in Japan? –(으)ㄴ 적/일 : has an/no experience to do something ( 있아 / 없다 ) Usually + -어/아 보다

front: 작년에 한국어를 배워 본 적이 있어요 .
back: I (had the experience) learned Korean last year. –(으)ㄴ 적/일 : has an/no experience to do something ( 있아 / 없다 ) Usually + -어/아 보다

front: 수잔이 사무실에서 일하 는 동안에 내가 로비에서 기다렸어요.
back: I waited in the lobby while Susan was working in the office. –는 동안 : while/during

front: 제가 없 는 동안에 남동생을 돌봐 주세요.
back: Please take care of my brother while I'm away. –는 동안 : while/during

front: 시험을 치 는 도중에 질문이 있으면 저한테 물으세요
back: If you have any questions during the exam, ask me. –는 도중 : on the way to/from / in the middle of

front: 집에 가 는 도중에 아버지를 만날 거예요
back: I'm meeting my father on the way home. –는 도중 : on the way to/from / in the middle of

front: 아마 영화를 보 는 도중에 잘 거예요.
back: I'll probably sleep during the movie. –는 도중 : on the way to/from / in the middle of

front: 도서관에서 그 책을 찾 는 중이에요
back: I'm in the middle of looking for a book at the library. –는 중이다 : in the middle of/in the process of
He's probably in the middle of driving. –는 중이다 : in the middle of/in the process of

front: 유니스가 저보다 수영장에 더 자주 가 는 편이에요 .
back: Eunice tends to go to the swimming pool more often than I do. –는/(으)ㄴ 편이다 : tends to/kind of

front: 나보다 형이 키가 더 큰 편이에요 .
back: My brother is kind of taller than me. –는/(으)ㄴ 편이다 : tends to/kind of
Do whatever you want. –는/(으)ㄴ 대로 : according to/the same/just as

front: 날이 밝 는 대로 떠날 거예요
back: I'm leaving as soon as dawn breaks. –는/(으)ㄴ 대로 : according to/the same/just as
Let's begin as soon as the weather gets better. –는/(으)ㄴ 대로 : according to/the same/just as

front: 아침을 먹 은 후에 보통 커피를 마셔요
back: I usually drink coffee after breakfast. –(으)ㄴ 후에 : afterward/later similar with ( 다음에 or 뒤 )

front: 숙제를 다 한 후에 운동할 거예요.
back: I will exercise after I finish my homework. –(으)ㄴ 후에 : afterward/later similar with ( 다음에 or 뒤 )

front: 운동을 한 다음에 보통 샤워를 해요.
back: I usually take a shower after exercising. –(으)ㄴ 후에 : afterward/later similar with ( 다음에 or 뒤 )
Write a review after watching the movie. –(으)ㄴ 후에 : afterward/later similar with ( 다음에 or 뒤 )

front: 엄마가 돌아오면 열심히 공부하 는 척할 거예요
back: I'm gonna pretend to study hard when she (mom) gets back. –는/(으)ㄴ 척/체하다 : pretend .divided between present and past

front: 친구를 만나면 슬 픈 척하 세요
back: Pretend to be sad when you meet your friend. –는/(으)ㄴ 척/체하다 : pretend .divided between present and past
I'll pretend to be thankful. –는/(으)ㄴ 척/체하다 : pretend .divided between present and past

front: 집을 사 는 바람에 돈이 없어요.
back: I have no money because I bought a house. –는 바람에 : as a result of/ because of (usually negative)

front: 늦게 일어나 는 바람에 학교에 못 갔어요.
back: I couldn't go to school becaues I woke up late. –는 바람에 : as a result of/ because of (usually negative)

front: 열쇠를 잃어 버리 는 바람에 약속을 못 지켰어요.
back: I lost my key and could not keep my appointment. –는 바람에 : as a result of/ because of (usually negative)
From now on, I'm running every day. –는/(으)ㄴ 것이다 : the fact is/what happened is that
The weather is warm today. –는/(으)ㄴ 것이다 : the fact is/what happened is that

front: 피곤해서 아침에 못 일어 날 뻔했어요
back: I was so tired and almost couldn't get up this morning. –(으)ㄹ 뻔했다 : almost/to be nearly

front: 공항에 늦게 도착해서 비행기를 놓 칠 뻔했어요 .
back: I almost missed my flight because I arrived at the airport late. –(으)ㄹ 뻔했다 : almost/to be nearly

front: 배가 아 플 때 이 약을 먹어요?
back: Do you take this medicine when you have a stomachache? -(으)ㄹ 때 : when

front: 로션을 밖에 나 갈 때 얼굴에 비르십시오.
back: Rub the lotion on your face when you go outside. -(으)ㄹ 때 : when
When I went to the libary I took the subway. -(으)ㄹ 때 : when

front: 그 책을 읽 을 수 있어요
back: I can read that book. –(으)ㄹ 수 있다/없다 : ability or possibility
I can't go to the party. –(으)ㄹ 수 있다/없다 : ability or possibility

front: 한국 음식을 만 들 줄 몰랐어요 .
back: I didn't know you could make Korean food. (or) I didn't know you would make Korean food. –(으)ㄹ 줄 알다/모르다 : know how to/ don’t know how to

front: 톰이 여기에 쓰레기를 버 릴 줄 몰랐어요
back: I didn't know Tom was gonna throw garbage here. –(으)ㄹ 줄 알다/모르다 : know how to/ don’t know how to

front: 여름에 코트를 입 을 리가 없어요 .
back: There's no way that you can wear a coat in the summer. –(으)ㄹ 리가 없다 : there is no possibility that

front: 그 친구들이 나를 싫어 할 리가 없어요.
back: There's no way those friends hate me. –(으)ㄹ 리가 없다 : there is no possibility that
Do you know what time Isabel is coming back? –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄴ지 : indirect question 알다 : (do you know?)

front: 데이빗이 요즘 얼마나 열심히 시험 공부를 하는지 아세요?
back: Do you know how hard David is studying for the exam these days? –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄴ지 : indirect question 알다 : (do you know?)
Do you know how difficult the exam was? –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄴ지 : indirect question 알다 : (do you know?)
I don't know where my older brother lives. –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄴ지 : indirect question 모르다 (speculative mindset)

front: 다음 학기에 어떤 수업을 들 을지 모르겠어요.
back: I don't know what classes I'm going to take next semester. –는/(으)ㄴ/(으)ㄴ지 : indirect question 모르다 (speculative mindset)

front: 그남자를 계속 만 나야 할지 모르겠어요 .
back: I don't know if I should keep seeing him. –어/아야 할지 모르다 : uncertain about what one must do

front: 먼저 무엇을 사 야 할지 모르겠어요 .
back: I don't know what to buy first. –어/아야 할지 모르다 : uncertain about what one must do

front: 가을 학기가 시작한 지 삼일이 지났어요 .
back: It's been three days since the fall semester started. –(으)s지..되다/지나다 : since / amount of time since a certain point
How long have you been learning golf? –(으)s지..되다/지나다 : since / amount of time since a certain point
The house was really huge you know? –더라구요 : stress authenticity of speaker experience / you know?

front: 건강을 위해서 매일 야채만 먹 더라구요 .
back: Everyday, he only eats vegetables for his health (you know) –더라구요 : stress authenticity of speaker experience / you know?
The person who used to read the newspaper. –던 : used to / habitual action in the past
Flowers that used to be pretty –던 : used to / habitual action in the past
